observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We	recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer shall send a written notice to the aforementioned payees for them to be aware of their stale checks. Henceforth, practice sending written notice to the payees one month before the check becomes stale as required in Section 104 of GAM for LGUs.
If checks are returned by the payee, the Municipal Accountant shall request a certified copy of the DV from the Auditor for presentation to the Municipal Mayor who shall countersign the check. The cancelled check shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation. The replacement check shall also be reported chronologically in the RCI.
If the payees are untraceable or did not respond to the notice, the Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary adjusting entries as follows:
For stale checks over 6 months but not over 2 years –
Cash in Bank-LCCA                             P xxxxx
Liability Account                                              P xxxxx
For stale checks over 2 years (in compliance with Section 97 of Presidential Decree No. 1445)–
Cash in Bank-LCCA					    P xxxxx
Prior Years Adjustment*					 P xxxxx
(*to represent surplus adjustment)

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Sangguniang Bayan a list of cash in bank accounts that remained dormant for so many years with the request to close and transfer them to the General Fund.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to close and transfer the said balances from the Trust Fund bank accounts to the General Fund bank accounts.
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to consider the balances as source in the preparation of the budget.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Re-activate the Inventory and Appraisal Committee to identify and appraise all properties whose costs cannot be reliably determined due to the unavailability of financial records. Require the committee to prepare an Appraisal Report and submit it to the Commission on Audit for Technical Review and Inspection.
Direct the Inventory Committee to dutifully conduct the annual physical count of all the property, plant, and equipment owned by the Municipality and to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE per COA Circular No. 2020-06.
Direct the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards, respectively, and to reconcile these with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of PPE in the financial statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to conduct assessment on both the physical and price changes of the biological asset and submit a report to the Municipal Accountant.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to re-measure biological asset based on the assessment of the Municipal Agriculturist and record the derived measurement in the books.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to record the capitalization of borrowing costs as part of the cost of the projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the full disclosure requirement under IPSAS 30 for the financial liabilities in the Notes to Financial Statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Municipal Engineer to hasten the implementation and completion of the subject development projects thru efficient monitoring so that the interest expenses incurred could be recouped thru the earnings that will be generated when these projects will be put into use.
The BAC to properly plan the procurement process of all identified priority development projects under the 20% DF to guarantee the prompt accomplishment of the same.
The Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant for the computation of penalties or liquidating damages for the delayed projects so that the contractor may expedite the completion of the projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to install signboards with complete project details on all Infrastructure Projects implemented by Administration and to require the winning Contractor on projects that underwent bidding to do the same pursuant to the existing regulations.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Convene the Municipal Officials concerned to strategically discuss the processes and procedures in establishing a Water Collecting Office.
Enjoin the Sangguniang Bayan to support the proposal.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Implementing Offices concerned to prepare and submit a monitoring and evaluation report of the subject projects.
The Municipal Engineer to require the contractors concerned to submit explanation on the incurrence of delays in completing the projects and subsequently determine the appropriate action to be taken whether to issue a Warning Letter or to directly employ punitive actions prescribed by existing guidelines.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the MDRRMO to coordinate with the MDC in formulating the Municipality’s DRRM Implementing Plan.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the HRMO must ensure that the functions and duties of the CoS/Job Order personnel must be well-defined and clear in the contract/job order, known and accepted by them prior to rendition of services to facilitate proper evaluation of work performed by these personnel; and, accomplishment reports should be evaluated vis-a-vis the terms/ services agreed upon in the contract.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Facilitate the establishment of the GAD M&E system as required in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to help the officials concerned in the proper monitoring of the implementation of GAD policies and PPAs and the evaluation of the effectiveness of the same;
Direct the Officials concerned to ensure proper mainstreaming of GAD concerns in the local development plans, especially in the CDP.
Direct the GAD Focal Point Person to furnish the Audit Team with reports required in JMC 2013-01 to aid in the audit of GAD funds and PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We commend the Municipality of Don  Marcelino  for  successfully  coming  up
with a contact  investigation  plan  which  will  be  the  anchor  to  guide
personnel in conducting contact tracing.

Likewise, we recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:


a) To address deficiencies in  Monitoring  of  Close  Contacts  at  barangay
  level


    • Direct the Local Contact Tracing Team to  guide  the  barangay  Health
      Emergency Response Team in conducting daily monitoring of the COVID-19
      patient’s contacts.

b) To address deficiencies in the Issuance  of  Certificate  of  Quarantine
  Completion

    • Direct the Municipal Health Officer to require the submission of daily
      monitoring forms and status of individuals subjected to quarantine and
      the corresponding recommendation from the Municipal  Epidemiology  and
      Surveillance  Unit  prior  to  the  issuance  of  the  Certificate  of
      Quarantine Completion.

c) To address deficiencies in the roles and responsibilities  of  the  Local
Government Units and officers concerned

    • Local Government Unit and Municipal Epidemiology and Surveillance Unit

    • Direct the MESU to prepare a list of documents submitted to  MESU  and
      subsequently archive the same for later reference and retrieval.


    • Require the MHO to identify the personnel in-charge of  each  activity
      in the case  investigation  plan  and  disseminate  the  same  to  the
      personnel in-charge.


    • Local Contact Tracing Team Leader and Co-Team Leader


    • Direct the Local Contact Tracing Team Leader ad  Co-  Team  Leader  to
      devise a contact tracing plan to be followed in the subsequent contact
      tracing activities.


    • Require the LCCT Leader and Co- Team Leader to direct the  members  of
      the Local Contact Tracing Team to provide daily report for evaluation,
      consolidation and submission to the MESU.


    • Instruct the LCCT Leader and Co- Team Leader  to  conduct  a  periodic
      trainings or orientation for the members of the Local Contact  Tracing
      Team to keep them abreast to the latest developments in healthcare and
      other protocols in handling COVID-19.


    • Remind the LCCT Leader and Co- Team Leader to utilize the  Demographic
      Vulnerability Tool of the DOH in planning and implementation of tasks.


    • Members of the Local Contact Tracing team


    • Require the members of the  Local  Contact  Tracing  Team  to  provide
      health education to those close contacts of individuals diagnosed with
      COVID-19 and document the same for reference and reporting.


    • Direct the members of the Local Contact tracing Team to conduct  daily
      monitoring of close contacts for 14 days and submit  the  accomplished
      daily forms to the Local Contact tracing  Team  Leader  and  Co-  Team
      Leader for consolidation and submission to the MESU

d) To address recording and reporting deficiencies


    • Require the MESU and Local Contact Tracing Team to observe the  timely
      submission of daily reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Reactivate the Inventory and Appraisal Committee to identify and appraise all properties whose cost cannot be reliably determined due to the unavailability of financial records. Require the committee to prepare an Appraisal Report and submit it to the Commission on Audit for Technical Review and Inspection.
Direct the Inventory Committee to dutifully conduct the annual physical count of all the property, plant and equipment owned by the Municipality and to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE per COA Circular No. 2020-06.
Direct the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards, respectively, and to reconcile these with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of PPE in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to compute for the proper depreciation of the PPE items through the preparation of a lapsing schedule and to subsequently prepare the corresponding adjusting entry.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct:
The Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory system in recording purchases of supplies and materials as provided for under GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and to subsequently prepare the corresponding adjusting entry to take up the requisitions and issuances.
The GSO to require the different Municipal Departments/Offices to prepare a summary of supplies and materials issued for consolidation by the GSO and submission to the Office of the Municipal Accountant as required under GAM for LGUs, Volume I. Conduct physical inventory of all supplies and materials by type, as required under Section 190 of the same Manual.
The GSO and the Accountant to oversee the conduct of the inventory process regularly to ensure control, monitoring and accounting of all inventory items and to accurately report the balances in the Municipality’s financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant must trace the transactions affecting the accounts to ascertain the correct composition of the accounts and take up the necessary adjusting journal entries in the appropriate books so that the accounts will be properly recorded and reported in the financial reports.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Cause the immediate liquidation of outstanding cash advances by the accountable officers concerned, especially those advances that are long overdue.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the salary of accountable officers or impose a refund if liquidation is not anymore possible.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
Stop the grant of cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than their official travels.

We  recommended  the  Municipality  of  Don   Marcelino   for   successfully
developing a contact investigation plan which will be the  anchor  to  guide
personnel in conducting investigation of COVID-19 cases.

Nonetheless, we recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following  activities
to improve the contact tracing activities of the Municipality:

a) Local Government Unit and Municipal Epidemiology and Surveillance Unit

    • Direct the MESU to prepare a list of documents submitted to  MESU  and
      subsequently archive the same for later reference and retrieval.



    • Require the MHO to identify the personnel in-charge of  each  activity
      in the case  investigation  plan  and  disseminate  the  same  to  the
      personnel in-charge.


b) Local Contact Tracing Team Leader and Co-Team Leader


    • Direct the Local Contact Tracing Team Leader ad  Co-  Team  Leader  to
      devise a contact tracing plan to be followed in the subsequent contact
      tracing activities.


    • Require the LCCT Leader and Co- Team Leader to direct the  members  of
      the Local Contact Tracing Team to provide daily report for evaluation,
      consolidation and submission to the MESU.

    • Instruct the LCCT Leader and Co- Team Leader  to  conduct  a  periodic
      trainings or orientation for the members of the Local Contact  Tracing
      Team to keep them abreast to the latest developments in healthcare and
      other protocols in handling COVID-19.

    • Remind the LCCT Leader and Co- Team Leader to utilize the  Demographic
      Vulnerability Tool of the DOH in planning and implementation of tasks.

c) Members of the Local Contact Tracing team


    • Require the members of the  Local  Contact  Tracing  Team  to  provide
      health education to those close contacts of individuals diagnosed with
      COVID-19 and document the same for reference and reporting.


    • Direct the members of the Local Contact tracing Team to conduct  daily
      monitoring of close contacts for 14 days and submit  the  accomplished
      daily forms to the Local Contact tracing  Team  Leader  and  Co-  Team
      Leader for consolidation and submission to the MESU.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the MSWD Office to identify the equipment needed for the
documentation of activities and events undertaken by the Municipality and to
facilitate its procurement, if warranted, through the Bids and Awards
Committee;
b. Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to take
cognizance with the guidelines issued by the regulatory agencies concerned
which affect the operations of the MSWD Office through periodically viewing
the portal of these agencies or through other activities that may be deemed
appropriate; and,
c. Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and the
Municipal Accountant to submit the OSCA Identification of the individuals
concerned to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the MSWD Office to device a process flow of transaction within their
office and facilitate the inclusion of review mechanisms in each stage of the
process; and,
b. Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to submit the
following: (a) Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the LGU and
DSWD; (b) copy of the valid identification of the recipients noted without
documents attached; and, (c) photographs of the recipients during the actual
receipt of the assistance.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the members of the Local COVID-19 Task Force to prepare the Local
COVID-19 Task Force Daily Report and submit the same to the regulatory
agencies;
b. Require the Municipal Health Office and the Contact Tracing Teams to provide
timely and accurate information to the Local COVID-19 Task Force for the
preparation of the report; and,
c. Instruct the Municipal Health Office, the members of the Local COVID-9 Task
Force and the Contact Tracing teams to keep abreast with the issuances and
requirements of regulatory agencies by attending conferences, browsing agency
websites or undertaking other means that may be deemed appropriate.

V. Recommendations:
We recommended the following to the Municipal Mayor:
a. To address the deficiencies in Monitoring Close Contacts at barangay level
• Direct the Local Contact Tracing Team to guide the barangay Health
Emergency Response Team in conducting daily monitoring of the COVID-
19 patient’s contacts.
b. To address the deficiencies in the Issuance of Certificate of Quarantine
Completion
• Direct the Municipal Health Officer to require the submission of daily
monitoring forms and status of individuals subjected to quarantine and the
corresponding recommendation from the Municipal Epidemiology and
Surveillance Unit prior to the issuance of the Certificate of Quarantine
Completion.

V. Recommendations:
We commended the Municipality of Don Marcelino for successfully developing a
contact investigation plan which will be the anchor to guide personnel in conducting
investigation of COVID-19 cases.
Nonetheless, we recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following activities to
improve the contact tracing activities of the Municipality:
a. Local Government Unit and Municipal Epidemiology and Surveillance Unit
• Direct the MESU to prepare a list of documents submitted to MESU and
subsequently archive the same for later reference and retrieval.
• Require the MHO to identify the personnel in-charge of each activity in the
case investigation plan and disseminate the same to the personnel in-charge.
b. Local Contact Tracing Team Leader and Co-Team Leader
• Direct the Local Contact Tracing Team Leader ad Co- Team Leader to devise
a contact tracing plan to be followed in the subsequent contact tracing
activities.
• Require the LCCT Leader and Co- Team Leader to direct the members of
the Local Contact Tracing Team to provide daily report for evaluation,
consolidation and submission to the MESU.
• Instruct the LCCT Leader and Co- Team Leader to conduct a periodic
trainings or orientation for the members of the Local Contact Tracing Team
to keep them abreast to the latest developments in healthcare and other
protocols in handling COVID-19.
• Remind the LCCT Leader and Co- Team Leader to utilize the Demographic
Vulnerability Tool of the DOH in planning and implementation of tasks.
c. Members of the Local Contact Tracing Team
• Require the members of the Local Contact Tracing Team to provide health
education to those close contacts of individuals diagnosed with COVID-19
and document the same for reference and reporting.
• Direct the members of the Local Contact Tracing Team to conduct daily
monitoring of close contacts for 14 days and submit the accomplished daily
forms to the Local Contact tracing Team Leader and Co- Team Leader for
consolidation and submission to the MESU.

V. Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Health Officer
to direct the MESU, Local Contact Tracing Team and BHERTs to observe the timely
submission of daily reports and to establish clear delegation of task and responsibilities in
accordance with existing guidelines.

IV. Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. To address the budgetary constraints:
• Require the Municipal Health Office, Municipal Engineering Office,
Municipal Budget Office, and other offices concerned, to identify the
funding required after duly considering the necessary activities for the
operation of COVID-19 facility;
• Request the Sangguniang Bayan to provide budget augmentations, if
necessary, to fund the activities;
• If there are funding gaps, these shall be submitted to Provincial
Government of Davao Occidental for resolution in coordination with
DOH, DILG and the DBM as provided in DOH-DILG JAO No. 2020-001.
b. To comply with the Infrastructure Requirements:
• Instruct the Municipal Health Office to provide for the necessary
specifications required in the COVID-19 facility infrastructures;
• Require the Municipal Engineering Office to provide for: (a) a
construction plan duly considering the specifications submitted by the
Municipal Health Office; (b) total estimated cost for the facility; (c) the
submission of PPMP to the Bids and Awards Committee intended for the
procurement of materials; and,
• Request the BAC to facilitate the expeditious procurement of the needed
materials for the construction.
c. To comply with the Supplies and Other Resources Requirements:
• Instruct the Municipal Health Office to: (a) identify the supplies and other
resources needed for the operation of the COVID-19 facilities; and, (b)
submit the PPMP to the Bids and Awards Committee intended for the
procurement of materials; and,
• Request the BAC to facilitate the expeditious procurement of the supplies
and other resources needed for the operation of the COVID-19 facilities.
d. To comply with the Service Requirements:
• Coordinate with the Department of Health, through the Municipal Health
Office, in providing for mental health and psychosocial support services
(MHPSS) in COVID-19 facilities as required under DOH-DILG JAO No.
2020-001.

III. Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. To comply with the Staffing Requirements:
• Require the Municipal Health Office to: (a) identify the staffing
requirements for the COVID-19 facilities; (b) provide for the estimated
funding requirements; and, (c) conduct pre-deployment orientations and
basic trainings to the health care providers;
• Instruct the Municipal Human Resource Office with the coordination of the
Municipal Health Office to facilitate the expeditious recruitment of staff for
the COVID-19 facilities; and,
• Request the Department of Health, with the coordination of the Municipal
Health Office, for the deployment of deputized doctors and nurses required
for the operation of the COVID-19 facility.
b. To comply with the Reportorial Requirements:
• Instruct the COVID-19 facility manager, through the Municipal Health
Office to prepare and submit the draft daily brief reports and submit the
same to the offices concerned;
• Require the Municipal Health Office to ensure that registration in the
Temporary Treatment and Monitoring Facility(TTMF) Tracker is
undertaken; and,
• Ensure that the DDC TTMF Application is updated.

V. Recommendations:
We recommended the following to the Municipal Mayor:
• Direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer and the
Local Solid Waste Management Board to conduct monitoring on each
barangay to ensure that COVID-19 related health care wastes are
segregated at the household level;
• Request the Barangays under the Municipality to conduct an information drive
to raise awareness of households on proper hygiene practices, and safe
handling of infectious and contaminated health care wastes for collection; and,
• Provide documentation on the segregation and storage activities for reference
and archiving.

V. Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer and the
Local Solid Waste Management Board to specify in the guidelines on waste
management the important details on the collection and transportation of
COVID-19 related health care waste including, but not limited to the following:
(a) manner of disinfection of collection and storage bins for COVID-19 related
health care waste; (b) schedule of collection of COVID-19 related health care
waste; and, (c) the prohibition of the manual segregation of COVID-19 related
health care wastes;
b. Instruct the Municipal Health Officer and the Municipal Environment and
Natural Resources Officer to conduct an orientation to waste collectors on the
proper handling of COVID-19 related health care wastes; and,
c. Provide documentation on collection and transportation activities for reference
and archiving.

V. Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to submit the
number of kilograms of COVID-19 related health care wastes for the period
covered September 11, 2020 to September 14, 2020;
b. Direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer and Municipal
Health Officer to submit the microbial laboratory tests results of the treatment
facility on COVID-19 related health care wastes to the DENR and/ or DOH for
approval;
c. Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to conduct
an orientation to waste disposal personnel on the classification of wastes being
delivered for precautionary measures, maintaining a safe distance from, and
avoid direct contact with the wastes; and,
d. Provide documentation on treatment and disposal activities for reference and
archiving.

V. Recommendations:
We commended the Municipal Mayor for establishing a composite team in the
Local Solid Waste Management Board which shall take the lead in the management of the
COVID-19 related health care wastes.
On the other hand, we recommended the following undertakings to the Municipal
Mayor to efficiently and effectively manage the COVID-19 related health care wastes:
a. Require the Municipal Environmental and Natural Resources Officer and Local
Solid Waste Management Board to prepare a COVID-19 waste management
plan and integrate the same to the 10- year Solid Waste Management Plan of
the Municipality;
b. Direct the MENRO to conduct orientation and other information drive about
the proper management of COVID-19 related wastes in barangays; and,
c. Require the MENRO to devise activities to support the Barangay Ecological
Solid Waste Management Committees in the proper handling of COVID-19
related health care wastes.

V. Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
• Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer and the
Municipal Health Officer to prepare a specific Occupational Health and Safety
Plan duly considering the risks faced by each department in the Municipality
and to submit the same to the Office of the Municipal Mayor for approval and
dissemination;
• Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer and the
Municipal Health Officer to conduct: (a) orientation on the proper usage and
maintenance of PPEs; and (b) trainings on Occupational Health and Safety with
emphasis on the hazards in the workplace, environmental and administrative
controls and the properties and characteristics of PPEs;
• Direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resource Officer to provide
disinfectants and hand sanitizers for every waste collection vehicle; and,
• Provide documentation of activities on Occupational Health and Safety Plan
implementation for reference and archiving.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer, Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and the Municipal
Accountant to submit the complete list of beneficiaries of the food relief packs;
and,
b. Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer, Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and the Municipal
Accountant to evaluate the policies on the flow of documents and monitoring

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and the

b. Direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to keep abreast

Recommendations:

b. Direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to keep abreast

V. Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Disaster Risk

of the storage facility that warrants improvement.

To sustain the successful implementation of the program, we recommended to the

Prepare a DRRM Implementing Plan containing the courses of actions before, during and after the calamity.

We recommended that in the allocation and utilization of Special Education Fund, the Local School Board shall give priority to programs, activities and projects enumerated in Sec. 272 of LGU Code, Art. 363(C) of IRR-LGU Code, DECS/DBM/DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, series of 1998 dated April 14, 1998 and 01-A as amended.

We recommended the following courses of action:
Strictly comply the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure transparency, equity, efficiency, and economy in government procurement and to avoid sanction on the circumvention of the rules and regulations. Henceforth, persons responsible for the conduct of procurement not in conformity with R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR shall explain in writing why fuel payments totalling to P3,479,677.50 will not be subjected to appropriate notices; and
Strengthen the procurement function of the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that all the processes and procedures in the procurement of goods are strictly followed and to preclude irregular transactions.

Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting/adjusting entries in the books to record the procurement of supplies and materials to its appropriate inventory accounts.  Promote the usage and maintenance of supply ledger cards. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive will direct the General Services Office, in coordination with the Accounting Office, to:
Create an Inventory Committee that will be responsible for the conduct of physical count.

Prepare promptly the Report on the Physical Count of PPE.
Submit religiously the RPCPPE to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year.
Create an Appraisal Committee to appraise all properties which cost cannot be reliably determined due to unavailability of financial records. Require the committee to prepare Appraisal Report and submit it to Commission on Audit for Technical Review and Inspection.
Completely maintain the prescribed Property Cards and PPELC, respectively and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the financial statements.

Effect the recording of all new accounts in the books.
